author: dkrupnov
title: Windows CI: CachingTest LoadMulti_race mock call count mismatch and Electron cleanup permission error
signature: CachingTest LoadMulti_race Expected import_model call count mismatch|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: C++ unit tests / C++ unit tests
JS API (22)
failed_jobs.symptom: Electron E2E cleanup fails: rm cannot remove electron.exe/d3dcompiler_47.dll - Permission denied

key_errors:
caching_test.cpp(2623): error: Actual function call count doesn't match EXPECT_CALL(*mockPlugin, import_model(A<std::istream&>(), _))...
  Expected: to be called 8 times
    Actual: called 7 times - unsatisfied and active
[  FAILED  ] CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMulti_race/ByModelName_testCache (2068 ms)

exec error: Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/d3dcompiler_47.dll': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/electron.exe': Permission denied
root_cause: Two independent transient failures on Windows: (1) CachingTest.LoadMulti_race is a multithreaded stress test that ran 171 iterations and failed due to a non-deterministic race condition in the inference caching layer - the mock plugin's import_model was called 7 instead of the expected 8 times due to timing-sensitive thread scheduling on the Windows runner. The test name explicitly includes 'race' indicating known sensitivity to timing. (2) The Electron E2E test teardown hook tries to rm -rf the demo-electron-app-project directory but Windows locks the electron.exe, d3dcompiler_47.dll, libEGL.dll, and libGLESv2.dll files because the Electron process is still running when cleanup executes, causing Permission Denied errors. Both failures are unrelated to the PR changes (memory_tests: update reporting).
reproduction_steps: N/A - both are timing-sensitive Windows-specific failures unlikely to reproduce on demand
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs - both failures are transient and unrelated to the PR changes
For the CachingTest race: consider adding retry logic or increasing tolerance in LoadMulti_race mock expectations
For the Electron cleanup: add a process kill step before rm -rf in the E2E test teardown, or use a Windows-safe directory removal approach
prevention_strategies: Mark CachingTest.LoadMulti_race as retryable or add exponential backoff. For Electron E2E cleanup, use taskkill /F /IM electron.exe before directory removal on Windows.
historical_context: First occurrence of this specific combination. Both failure types (Windows file lock and race conditions in caching tests) are common on Windows CI runners.
